screen_donor_acceptor_layout.purpose: Summarize donor-acceptor segmentation and conjugation-level layout from bounded structural evidence.

screen_rotor_torsion_topology.purpose: Summarize rotor density, torsion candidates, and branching topology from bounded structural evidence.

screen_planarity_compactness.purpose: Summarize planarity, compactness, principal span, and conformer-dispersion proxies.

screen_intramolecular_hbond_preorganizat.purpose: Inspect reusable prepared geometry for intramolecular H-bond and typed donor/acceptor preorganization cues.

screen_conformer_geometry_proxy.purpose: Summarize available conformer-level geometry proxies and single-conformer limitations.

screen_neutral_aromatic_structure.purpose: Summarize aromatic-core dominance, ring-system rigidity, and neutral-aromatic structural cues.

pending_agent_instructions.microscopic: Current reasoning phase: portfolio_screening. Agent framing mode: portfolio_neutral. The current_hypothesis 'neutral aromatic' is only a provisional top1 for bookkeeping in this stage. Do not anchor this microscopic task to it as if the mechanism were settled. Screening focus hypotheses: TICT, unknown. Screening focus summary: Current round is 20 of 20, so no follow-up round remains. The new macro evidence increases support for neutral aromatic and weakens TICT indirectly, but by rule the case cannot enter pairwise contraction because TICT and unknown still remain in coverage debt with only indirect screening status. Portfolio screening therefore remains incomplete at the round limit. Task: Collect additional microscopic evidence for the current working hypothesis 'neutral aromatic'. Focus on the current gap: A direct discriminative screen for torsion-coupled CT/localization sufficient either to directly screen TICT or to mark that route blocked by capability; this unresolved pairwise gap also keeps unknown in debt.  Keep the task low-cost and bounded to current microscopic capability. Do not enter pairwise contraction because coverage_debt_hypotheses is non-empty. TICT and unknown have not reached directly_screened, blocked_by_capability, or dropped_with_reason status. Since rounds_remaining_including_current is 1 in this round, there is no justified future follow-up to close the debt now. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point. Do not contract to a pure top1-vs-top2 closure until the remaining coverage debt is cleared.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Latest macro planarity/compactness evidence supports a highly planar, compact, aromatic-core-dominated hydrocarbon scaffold with no donor-acceptor segmentation, which is more compatible with neutral aromatic behavior than with a torsion-decoupled CT mechanism.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: TICT remains the main challenger because the system is still rotor-rich and conformer-sensitive, but the new high-planarity/high-compactness structural screen weakens the expectation of a strongly decoupled torsional CT state in the currently screened geometry regime.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Unknown stays nonzero because the neutral aromatic versus TICT pair is not directly closed and round budget is exhausted, but the evidence remains concentrated in that named pair rather than opening a new mechanism family.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: ICT remains low because donor-acceptor layout screening is directly unfavorable and the new planarity/compactness result does not create a conventional charge-separated scaffold.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: ESIPT remains structurally excluded because there are no hetero atoms or proton-transfer motifs.

hypothesis_screening_ledger.screening_note: TICT remains structurally credible because rotor richness and conformer sensitivity were observed, but the latest direct macro screen adds high planarity and compactness support that cuts against a strongly torsion-decoupled CT picture. No direct torsion-coupled CT/localization signature was obtained, so TICT debt remains unresolved rather than cleared.
Donor-acceptor layout remains directly unfavorable for ICT, and the new structural screen does not reopen this route.
No hetero atoms are present, so ESIPT remains structurally implausible.

Unknown remains reduced because the live competition is still concentrated in neutral aromatic versus TICT, and the latest macro evidence reinforces that named pair rather than suggesting a new mechanism family. It remains in debt because that pair is still not directly closed and the final round budget prevents another bounded direct screen.

Neutral aromatic is directly supported by aromatic-core structural screening, repeated baseline-compatible internal evidence, and now a deterministic planarity/compactness screen showing a highly planar, compact, ring-dominated hydrocarbon scaffold.

planner_diagnosis_history: The task is to assess the likely AIE mechanism for a very large polycyclic/polyphenyl hydrocarbon. The current top1 working hypothesis is neutral aromatic, and the current top2 runner-up is TICT. ICT remains a credible third candidate in the screening portfolio, while ESIPT is effectively excluded because the molecule contains no hetero atoms and thus lacks an obvious proton-transfer motif. Top1 is still only a working hypothesis because the present evidence is dominated by structure-derived priors: the molecule is highly aromatic, neutral, and donor-acceptor-poor, but it is also rich in peripheral aryl rotors and branching, so restriction-of-intramolecular-motion language could overlap with either a neutral aromatic AIE picture or a more torsion-mediated TICT-like interpretation. Shared prepared structure context is available and usable, with a selected conformer, but the initial microscopic policy only permits one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action and not conformer or torsion sensitivity. Macro can provide deterministic bounded structural screening from one registry-backed capability, but it cannot make a global mechanism decision. Microscopic can provide one baseline excited-state snapshot, but it cannot by itself settle full mechanism or explore multiple geometrical pathways in round 1. Therefore the first round should gather both macro and microscopic evidence: macro to screen rotor/torsion topology directly relevant to TICT-vs-neutral-aromatic coverage debt, and microscopic to obtain a bounded baseline S0/S1 readout that can indirectly assess whether strong charge-transfer character is even plausible. The case remains in portfolio screening rather than pairwise contraction because TICT and ICT have not yet been screened enough to justify collapsing the portfolio to only top1-vs-top2 closure.

Local reasoning summary: The Planner explicitly requires a first-round low-cost baseline S0/S1 microscopic task with no heavy DFT geometry optimization and no excited-state relaxation. The action_registry includes a "run_baseline_bundle" execution action that exactly matches this requirement, providing low-cost aTB1 S0 geometry optimization plus vertical excited-state manifold characterization as default deliverables. The task_mode baseline_s0_s1 mandates using "run_baseline_bundle". The shared prepared structure context is available and preferred. No discovery actions are needed. Therefore, the best supported single-action translation is to run the baseline bundle with new calculation and ground state optimization enabled.
Capability limit note: The microscopic worker cannot perform heavy full-DFT geometry optimization or excited-state relaxation at this phase. Only one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action is allowed. The action "run_baseline_bundle" fully supports the requested deliverables within these constraints.
Failure policy: If the baseline bundle calculation fails or is unavailable, no fallback action is defined in this microscopic context. The task would be reported as unsupported or failed at a higher level. No partial or proxy actions are appropriate given the hard binding to baseline_s0_s1 mode.

{
  "status": "supported",

  "params": {
    "perform_new_calculation": true,

}

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Latest macro planarity/compactness evidence supports a highly planar, compact, aromatic-core-dominated hydrocarbon scaffold with no donor-acceptor segmentation, which is more compatible with neutral aromatic behavior than with a torsion-decoupled CT mechanism.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: TICT remains the main challenger because the system is still rotor-rich and conformer-sensitive, but the new high-planarity/high-compactness structural screen weakens the expectation of a strongly decoupled torsional CT state in the currently screened geometry regime.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Unknown stays nonzero because the neutral aromatic versus TICT pair is not directly closed and round budget is exhausted, but the evidence remains concentrated in that named pair rather than opening a new mechanism family.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: ICT remains low because donor-acceptor layout screening is directly unfavorable and the new planarity/compactness result does not create a conventional charge-separated scaffold.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: ESIPT remains structurally excluded because there are no hetero atoms or proton-transfer motifs.

hypothesis_screening_ledger.screening_note: TICT remains structurally credible because rotor richness and conformer sensitivity were observed, but the latest direct macro screen adds high planarity and compactness support that cuts against a strongly torsion-decoupled CT picture. No direct torsion-coupled CT/localization signature was obtained, so TICT debt remains unresolved rather than cleared.
Donor-acceptor layout remains directly unfavorable for ICT, and the new structural screen does not reopen this route.
No hetero atoms are present, so ESIPT remains structurally implausible.

Unknown remains reduced because the live competition is still concentrated in neutral aromatic versus TICT, and the latest macro evidence reinforces that named pair rather than suggesting a new mechanism family. It remains in debt because that pair is still not directly closed and the final round budget prevents another bounded direct screen.

Neutral aromatic is directly supported by aromatic-core structural screening, repeated baseline-compatible internal evidence, and now a deterministic planarity/compactness screen showing a highly planar, compact, ring-dominated hydrocarbon scaffold.

An additional candidate, unknown, also decreases slightly because the evidence remains concentrated in the named neutral aromatic versus TICT competition rather than opening a distinct alternative. TICT and unknown still remain in coverage debt, so the case must stay in portfolio screening and cannot legitimately enter pairwise contraction. The key remaining discriminator is still a direct torsion-coupled CT/localization readout or an explicit blocked-by-capability determination for that route. The current top1 working hypothesis is neutral aromatic, and the current top2 runner-up is TICT. ICT remains a credible third candidate in the screening portfolio, while ESIPT is effectively excluded because the molecule contains no hetero atoms and thus lacks an obvious proton-transfer motif. Top1 is still only a working hypothesis because the present evidence is dominated by structure-derived priors: the molecule is highly aromatic, neutral, and donor-acceptor-poor, but it is also rich in peripheral aryl rotors and branching, so restriction-of-intramolecular-motion language could overlap with either a neutral aromatic AIE picture or a more torsion-mediated TICT-like interpretation. Shared prepared structure context is available and usable, with a selected conformer, but the initial microscopic policy only permits one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action and not conformer or torsion sensitivity. Macro can provide deterministic bounded structural screening from one registry-backed capability, but it cannot make a global mechanism decision. Microscopic can provide one baseline excited-state snapshot, but it cannot by itself settle full mechanism or explore multiple geometrical pathways in round 1. Therefore the first round should gather both macro and microscopic evidence: macro to screen rotor/torsion topology directly relevant to TICT-vs-neutral-aromatic coverage debt, and microscopic to obtain a bounded baseline S0/S1 readout that can indirectly assess whether strong charge-transfer character is even plausible. final_answer.diagnosis: Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.66) and top2 is TICT (0.23). The latest evidence strengthens the current top1: the new planarity/compactness screen reports a highly planar, compact, aromatic-core-dominated hydrocarbon with no hetero atoms and no donor-acceptor partition, which is more consistent with neutral aromatic behavior than with a torsion-enabled CT mechanism. The top2 challenger TICT is weakened but not eliminated, because rotor richness and conformer sensitivity still make it structurally credible, yet the new observable family cuts against the need for strong torsional decoupling. An additional candidate, unknown, also decreases slightly because the evidence remains concentrated in the named neutral aromatic versus TICT competition rather than opening a distinct alternative. TICT and unknown still remain in coverage debt, so the case must stay in portfolio screening and cannot legitimately enter pairwise contraction. The key remaining discriminator is still a direct torsion-coupled CT/localization readout or an explicit blocked-by-capability determination for that route. Because this is round 20 of 20, the best action now is not to pretend the case is pairwise-ready, but to record that portfolio screening ended with unresolved debt and no budget for another bounded direct screen.
final_answer.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Neutral aromatic is best supported overall, but uncertainty remains material because TICT was only indirectly weakened and never directly cleared; unknown remains residual because the unresolved competition never fully closed before the round budget expired.
final_answer.capability_assessment: Macro capabilities were the most reliable late-stage route and successfully added a new observable family. Verifier supplementation was repeatedly operationally stalled by credit/token failure, and parse-only microscopic CT extraction repeatedly failed to deliver direct CT/localization observables. A further microscopic route was also derailed once by protocol/action-schema mismatch. With no rounds remaining, capability limits prevent debt closure. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point. Current round is 20 of 20, so no follow-up round remains. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point.
